Featured Investment
February 2026 · 6 min read · 2,840 views

Owning a Second Home in the UAE: Why Global Citizens Are Buying in Dubai

Today, a growing number of international buyers are looking beyond traditional investments and choosing Dubai as their second home destination. From zero property tax and high rental yields to the Golden Visa pathway, Dubai offers a compelling package that very few global cities can match. With freehold ownership rights extended to all nationalities in designated zones, buying property in Dubai has never been more accessible — or more rewarding.

Zero property tax and no capital gains tax on real estate
Golden Visa eligibility for AED 2M+ property investments
Rental yields averaging 5–8% across prime Dubai locations
Freehold ownership open to all nationalities in 60+ zones
HomeKeys EditorialResearch & Market Analysis

January 2026·5 min read

How Cryptocurrency Is Changing Property Transactions in the UAE

Cryptocurrency is no longer just a buzzword — it's actively transforming how real estate works in the UAE. Bitcoin, Ethereum and stablecoins are now accepted by major developers including DAMAC and Emaar, processed through VARA-licensed exchanges providing full legal compliance. Blockchain-based smart contracts are cutting intermediary costs and enabling near-instant deal settlement.

BitcoinBlockchainVARAPropTech

January 2026·7 min read

Short-Term vs Long-Term Rentals in Dubai: Which Strategy Works Best?

Short-term rentals surged 18% in 2025, driven by Dubai's record 19.59 million tourist arrivals. Meanwhile, long-term leases delivered 13% steady growth in annual rental values. Our in-depth analysis breaks down ROI by location, property type, management costs and compliance requirements under DTCM and DLD — helping investors choose the right strategy for their portfolio.

AirbnbRental YieldROIDTCM

December 2025·5 min read

How Tourism Growth Is Reshaping Dubai's Property Prices in 2025

Tourism in Dubai is entering 2025 with unprecedented strength, driven by global events, new attractions and the city's status as a luxury travel capital. With over 19.59 million international visitors — a 5% year-on-year rise — the hospitality and real estate sectors are operating in near-perfect symbiosis, driving property values and short-term rental demand to record highs across the emirate.

Tourism 2025Property PricesHospitality

January 2026·4 min read

HomeKeys Supports DLD's New 'Ejari' Campaign to Boost Rental Transparency

The Dubai Land Department launched its landmark 'Ejari – Step by Step' awareness campaign to simplify rental processes for landlords, tenants and brokers across the city. The initiative covers lease registration, contract cancellation, certificate downloads and RERA rental increase calculations — all designed to reduce disputes and build a fully transparent rental ecosystem in Dubai.

EjariDLDRERATransparency

February 2026·8 min read

Top 5 Areas to Invest in Dubai Property in 2026

Dubai's property market continues to outperform global benchmarks with rental yields of 6–9% and strong capital appreciation heading into 2026. Our analysts break down the five best areas to invest right now: Dubai Marina, Downtown Dubai, Business Bay, Jumeirah Village Circle and the rapidly emerging Dubai Creek Harbour — with data-driven insights on yields, price trends and future growth potential for each.

November 2025·6 min read

The Rise of Smart Communities: Why Everyone's Talking About Expo City Dubai

Expo City Dubai has evolved from a post-event legacy project into one of the UAE's most sought-after residential and investment destinations. Designed as a blueprint for sustainable urban planning with net-zero carbon targets, it blends smart technology infrastructure, expansive green spaces and world-class metro connectivity — attracting both end-users and investors seeking the communities of tomorrow, available today.
